Robinia pseudoacacia, also known as Black locust, is a deciduous tree native to North America. The health benefits of Black locust include its ability to support cardiovascular health and its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Black locust include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Black locust include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support cardiovascular health and treat various other conditions.
